pytorch.reason: classical probability probes do not require autograd or tensor ops; numpy suffices for the admissibility geometry

pyg.reason: graph message-passing not needed; constraint manifold is 2D euclidean

z3.reason: SMT UNSAT proof that no (p,q) state can simultaneously satisfy the distinguishability constraint (|p-q|>eps) and the negation of it; UNSAT is the primary exclusion proof — not just empirical filtering

cvc5.reason: z3 covers the UNSAT requirement; cvc5 not added to avoid redundancy

sympy.reason: constraint geometry is numeric; no symbolic algebra needed

clifford.reason: no Clifford algebra structure in this 2D probability probe

geomstats.reason: constraint manifold is flat euclidean; no Riemannian structure needed

e3nn.reason: SO(3) equivariance not relevant to 2D probability constraint probe

rustworkx.reason: graph structure not part of this constraint manifold pipeline

xgi.reason: hypergraph not needed; constraints are pairwise scalar inequalities

toponetx.reason: cell-complex topology not required; gudhi covers the homology layer

gudhi.reason: RipsComplex + persistent homology on the admitted state cloud; Betti numbers (beta_0, beta_1) structurally characterise the topology of the admissibility region independent of numeric values

hypothesis.reason: Property-based generation of random (p, q) probe states across [0,1]^2; hypothesis shrinks counterexamples and confirms the exclusion predicate holds for all sampled states, not just hand-picked examples

pymoo.reason: NSGA-II Pareto optimisation over (topological complexity, constraint cost) objective pair; identifies non-dominated admitted states that trade off topological richness against proximity to the exclusion boundary

datasketch.reason: MinHash LSH clusters admitted states by Jaccard similarity of their discretized feature fingerprints; cluster representatives are Rosetta candidates — structurally distinct admitted states for further probing

pos_a_z3_excluded_states_unsat.note: All excluded states must yield UNSAT when asserted simultaneously admitted

pos_b_gudhi_topology.note: Admitted set must have fewer connected components than total states (non-trivial topology)

pos_c_pymoo_pareto_front.note: Pareto front must contain > 1 non-dominated solution

pos_d_datasketch_clusters.note: LSH must find >= 2 distinct clusters in the admitted state space

pos_e_admitted_subset.note: Admitted set must be non-empty but strictly smaller than total probe set

neg_a_identical_states_excluded.note: Identical states (p=q) must be excluded by the distinguishability constraint

neg_b_z3_identical_unsat.note: z3 must return UNSAT for p=q=0.5 when asserting simultaneous exclusion and admission

neg_c_gudhi_empty_set.note: Empty admitted set must yield Betti-0 = 0

neg_d_datasketch_single_cluster.note: Single inserted state must appear in its own query results

boundary_a_small_epsilon.note: Near-zero epsilon must admit > 80% of non-identical states

boundary_b_large_epsilon.note: Large epsilon (0.5) must admit < 30% of states (most excluded)

boundary_c_monotone_at_epsilon.note: States just below epsilon excluded; states just above admitted (strict inequality boundary)
